core_payload.content:
# CapsuleOS Stewardship Interfaces

## 1. Role
CapsuleOS Stewardship Interfaces owns the human and agent surfaces that inspect, navigate, and maintain sovereign memory during live runtime. It keeps chat ingress, swarm maintenance, orchestration, and graph reading together so operating-law stewardship remains visible and bounded.

## 2. Stewardship Interface Surface
This sub-hub groups `n-infinity`, `symphony`, `chat-to-capsules`, `vault-stewardship-swarm`, and `capsule-librarian-agent`. Together they define how humans and bounded workers inspect capsule law, route maintenance, navigate graph state, and continue upkeep without turning CapsuleOS into hidden background behavior.

## 3. Operating Boundary
This sub-hub does not define constitutional capsule law, trust-entry judgment, or storage and audit substrate. Those responsibilities belong to `law-surfaces`, `admission-lifecycle`, and `runtime-substrate`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: CapsuleOS Stewardship Interfaces groups the human and agent maintenance surfaces of the operating system: N-Infinity, Symphony, Chat to Capsules, Vault Stewardship Swarm, and Capsule Librarian Agent. It keeps graph reading, orchestration, chat ingress, and maintenance routing together so CapsuleOS stewardship remains visible and bounded during live runtime. This boundary matters because sovereign memory should be maintained through explicit interfaces rather than through hidden background behavior or scattered tool folklore. It gives upkeep one legible interface family during daily and nightly operation.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted during CapsuleOS decomposition to isolate the human and agent interfaces that inspect, navigate, and maintain sovereign memory.
